reason: The statement applies because Nehemiah, the protagonist in this text fragment, can be considered a righteous man who steps up in the face of challenges. He hears the cry of the people, confronts the nobles and rulers for their unjust practices, and advocates for the well-being of his brethren. He also leads by example, showing integrity in his leadership and dedication to serving the people. His actions reflect righteousness and courage in the face of adversity.
